\(W_d^*\) Null simulation figures and summary table for GitHub

Bashir Hamidi

2026-03-26

1 Preliminaries

1.1 Load packages

library(tidyverse); packageVersion("tidyverse")
## [1] '2.0.0'
library(reshape2); packageVersion("reshape2")
## [1] '1.4.5'
library(ggplot2); packageVersion("ggplot2")
## [1] '4.0.2'

1.2 Load data

df_raw <- df_raw %>% rename(., 
                                    "Adjusted Wd*" = "p_value_adist",
                                    "t-test" = "p_value_t_test")
(df_working <- df_raw)

1.3 Output directory

1.4 Simulation scenario

\To look at Type I error: \X <-> M -> Y

\Y independent of X | M 
\Xi = 0 if 1<i<=n0; 1 if n0+1<i<=n0+n1 
\Mi ~ N(b*Xi, sd=1) 
\Yi ~ N(d*Mi, sd=1) = d*N(b*Xi, sd=1) + N(0,sd=1)

\X (exposure) -> M (mediator) 
\M -> R (response) (b) 
\X -> R (d = 0)

\To look at power 
\X <-> M -> Y <-

2 Summarizing data

signif = 0.05

df_sum <- df_working %>% group_by(n0, n1, b, d) %>%
  summarise(`Adjusted Wd*` = mean(`Adjusted Wd*` < signif),
            `t-test` = mean(`t-test` < signif))
## `summarise()` has regrouped the output.
## ℹ Summaries were computed grouped by n0, n1, b, and d.
## ℹ Output is grouped by n0, n1, and b.
## ℹ Use `summarise(.groups = "drop_last")` to silence this message.
## ℹ Use `summarise(.by = c(n0, n1, b, d))` for per-operation grouping
##   (`?dplyr::dplyr_by`) instead.
# We went from 10.5 million to 1,050, reducing by a factor of 10k which is the number of repetitions we did for each scenario. 

df_sum_melted <- reshape2::melt(df_sum,
                         id.vars = c("n0", "n1", "b", "d"),
                         measure.vars = c("Adjusted Wd*", "t-test"),
                         variable.name = "Method",
                         value.name = "Reject")   
# went from two cols to 1 col so doubled the nrow

df_sum_melted$n = 
  df_sum_melted$n0 + 
  df_sum_melted$n1

df_sum_melted
df_sum_melted$balance <- with(df_sum_melted, n0/n1)

3 Save summary table for GitHub

Raw table with 10.5 million observations (10.5M rows and 7 columns) is 373 MB, which is beyond the the limits for GitHub files.

We will preserve the raw data table in our records and share the summary table (10.5M/10k replicates = 1050 observations). This is more manageable for sharing on GitHub.

saveRDS(df_sum_melted, 
        file = paste0(output_dir, "Adjusted WdS_null simulation_summary table.rds"))

4 binomial tests

4.0.1 One sided greater

This is for 1k reps

binom.test(58, 1000, p=0.05, alternative="greater") 
## 
##  Exact binomial test
## 
## data:  58 and 1000
## number of successes = 58, number of trials = 1000, p-value = 0.1389
## alternative hypothesis: true probability of success is greater than 0.05
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.04632358 1.00000000
## sample estimates:
## probability of success 
##                  0.058
binom.test(59, 1000, p=0.05, alternative="greater") 
## 
##  Exact binomial test
## 
## data:  59 and 1000
## number of successes = 59, number of trials = 1000, p-value = 0.1106
## alternative hypothesis: true probability of success is greater than 0.05
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.04722347 1.00000000
## sample estimates:
## probability of success 
##                  0.059
binom.test(60, 1000, p=0.05, alternative="greater") 
## 
##  Exact binomial test
## 
## data:  60 and 1000
## number of successes = 60, number of trials = 1000, p-value = 0.08673
## alternative hypothesis: true probability of success is greater than 0.05
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.04812436 1.00000000
## sample estimates:
## probability of success 
##                   0.06
binom.test(61, 1000, p=0.05, alternative="greater") 
## 
##  Exact binomial test
## 
## data:  61 and 1000
## number of successes = 61, number of trials = 1000, p-value = 0.06706
## alternative hypothesis: true probability of success is greater than 0.05
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.04902623 1.00000000
## sample estimates:
## probability of success 
##                  0.061
binom.test(62, 1000, p=0.05, alternative="greater") 
## 
##  Exact binomial test
## 
## data:  62 and 1000
## number of successes = 62, number of trials = 1000, p-value = 0.05111
## alternative hypothesis: true probability of success is greater than 0.05
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.04992905 1.00000000
## sample estimates:
## probability of success 
##                  0.062
binom.test(63, 1000, p=0.05, alternative="greater") 
## 
##  Exact binomial test
## 
## data:  63 and 1000
## number of successes = 63, number of trials = 1000, p-value = 0.03839
## alternative hypothesis: true probability of success is greater than 0.05
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.0508328 1.0000000
## sample estimates:
## probability of success 
##                  0.063

This is for 10k reps

binom.test(533, 10000, p=0.05, alternative="greater")
## 
##  Exact binomial test
## 
## data:  533 and 10000
## number of successes = 533, number of trials = 10000, p-value = 0.06902
## alternative hypothesis: true probability of success is greater than 0.05
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.04965182 1.00000000
## sample estimates:
## probability of success

5 Figure 2

subset(method==Wd*,
balance==1.00)
x = n/2
color = b
shape = d
facets = NA

chunk_plot_name <- "Figure_2"
df_sum_melted2 <- df_sum_melted %>%
  mutate(line_label_1 = ifelse(b==1 & d==2, "Nominal Type I error rate", NA), 
         line_label_2 = ifelse(b==1 & d==2, "Lower bound CI if α > 0.5", NA)) # Effective rejection rate
# lower bound for the confidence intervals if the rejection rate is 0inflated greater than 0.05.

chunk_plot <-
  ggplot(subset(df_sum_melted2,
                balance==1.00 &
                Method=="Adjusted Wd*" &
                b %in% c(.2, .5, 1, 2) &
                d %in% c(.2, .5, 1, 2)  ),
         aes(x=(n/2), y=Reject, group=interaction(b, d))) +#, group=interaction(Method, (d)))) +
  geom_point(aes(shape=factor(d), color=factor(b),  stroke=1), size=3.5, alpha=0.8) + 

  geom_line(aes(color=factor(b)), size=1, alpha=0.8 ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = signif, color = "maroon") +
    geom_text(size=7, color="maroon", aes(0, signif, label=line_label_1, hjust=-1.6, vjust=-.43)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.0536, lty = "dashed", color="black")+
    geom_text(size=7, color="black", aes(0, 0.0533, label=line_label_2, hjust=-1.59, vjust=-.54)) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off")+ # Prevents cutting off of labels
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = expression(paste("Simulation under the null")))+ #Adjusted W[d]^{*}  #$W_d^*$
     ylab("Rejection Rate") + 
     xlab("Per-Group Sample Size")+
  theme(plot.background= element_rect(fill=NULL),
            legend.position="bottom",
            legend.byrow=TRUE,# whether the legend-matrix is filled by columns (FALSE, the default) or by rows (TRUE).
            legend.title=element_text(size=25),
            legend.text=element_text(size=25),
            text=element_text(size=25),  #size of xlab and ylab, also size of strip.text.y but but latter is changed by a ratio
            axis.text.x=element_text(size=20),  #grid/wrap tick label size
            axis.text.y=element_text(size=20)#, #grid/wrap tick label size
            ) +
 # guides(shape=guide_legend(title.position = "top", override.aes = list(size=5, stroke=4), order=1),
 #          color=guide_legend(title.position = "top", override.aes = list(size=5, stroke=4), order=2)
 #          )+
 # scale_x_discrete(limits=c(10,200), breaks = c(10, 20, 40, 100, 200), labels = c("10", "20", "40", "100", "200"), name = "Sample Size Total")#+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=c(5, 10, 20, 50, 100), minor_breaks=NULL) +  #seq(-3,2,0.5)
  #scale_y_continuous(breaks=c(0, .01, .02, .03, .04, .05), minor_breaks=NULL)   #seq(-3,2,0.5)
#scale_x_discrete(limits=c(0.5, 2), breaks = c(0.5,1,2), labels = c(".5","1","2")) +
  
 scale_shape_discrete(name="M-Y Effect (d)")+ #values=c(21, 24, 22, 23), #+
 scale_color_discrete(name="X-M Effect (b)")+#, #values=c("#F8766D", "#00BA38"),# "#619CFF"), 
 #                    labels=c("aWd*", "t-test"))#+
 guides(shape=guide_legend(title.position = "top", override.aes = list(size=4, stroke=1.4), order=1),
         color=guide_legend(title.position = "top", override.aes = list(size=4, stroke=1.4), order=2)
         ) 
## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
png(paste0(output_dir, (chunk_plot_name),".png"), width=12, height=12, units="in", res=400)#, width=18, height=14)
print(chunk_plot)
## Warning: Removed 75 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_text()`).
## Warning: Removed 75 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_text()`).
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2
#dev.off(dev.list()["png"])
print(chunk_plot)
## Warning: Removed 75 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_text()`).
## Removed 75 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_text()`).

6 Figure S1

subset(balance==1.00)
x = n/2
color = method 
facets = d.b

chunk_plot_name <- "Figure_S1"

df_sum_melted2 <- df_sum_melted %>%
  mutate(line_label_1 = ifelse(b==1 & d==2, "Nominal Type I error rate", NA), 
         line_label_2 = ifelse(b==1 & d==2, "Lower bound CI if α > 0.5", NA)) # Effective rejection rate
# lower bound for the confidence intervals if the rejection rate is inflated greater than 0.05.

chunk_plot <-
  ggplot(subset(df_sum_melted2, balance==1.00), 
      aes(x=(n/2), y=Reject, group=Method))+#, group=interaction(Method, (d)))) +
  geom_point(aes(color=Method, stroke=1), size=3, alpha=0.7) + 
  geom_line(aes(color=Method), size=1, alpha=0.8) + 
  #geom_point(aes(shape=factor(n1), color=factor(Method), fill=alpha("white", 0.2), size=3)) + 
  #geom_line() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = signif, color = "maroon") +
    geom_text(size=5.7, color="maroon", aes(0, signif, label=line_label_1, hjust=0.02, vjust=1.3)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.0536, lty = "dashed", color="black")+
    geom_text(size=5.7, color="black", aes(0, 0.0533, label=line_label_2, hjust=0.02, vjust=-.9)) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") + # Prevents cutting off of labels
#    geom_text(aes(0,0.061,label = "Expected rejection", vjust = -1))+
 #scale_x_discrete("n") +
  #scale_shape_discrete("Method") +
  #scale_color_discrete("Method", limits=c("p_value_adist", "p_value_t_test"), breaks=c("p_value_adist", "p_value_t_test"), labels=c("aWd*", "t-test")) +
#  scale_shape_discrete("d (magnitude of M on Y)") +
#  scale_x_continuous(limits=c(0,1.5), breaks = c(0.2,0.5,1,1.5), labels = c(".2",".5","1","1.5")) +

  #  facet_grid(dhet ~ balance, scale="free") +
  facet_grid(d~b, labeller=label_both)+
  #facet_grid(n1~n0, scale="fixed", labeller=label_both) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = expression(paste("Simulation under the null")))+ #Adjusted W[d]^{*}  #$W_d^*$
     ylab("Rejection Rate") + 
     xlab("Per-Group Sample Size")+
  theme(plot.background= element_rect(fill=NULL),
            legend.position="bottom",
            legend.byrow=TRUE,# whether the legend-matrix is filled by columns (FALSE, the default) or by rows (TRUE).
            legend.title=element_text(size=20),
            legend.text=element_text(size=20),
            text=element_text(size=20),  #size of xlab and ylab, also size of strip.text.y but but latter is changed by a ratio
            axis.text.x=element_text(size=15),  #grid/wrap tick label size
            axis.text.y=element_text(size=20)#, #grid/wrap tick label size
            ) +
 # guides(shape=guide_legend(title.position = "top", override.aes = list(size=5, stroke=4), order=1),
 #          color=guide_legend(title.position = "top", override.aes = list(size=5, stroke=4), order=2)
 #          )+
 # scale_x_discrete(limits=c(10,200), breaks = c(10, 20, 40, 100, 200), labels = c("10", "20", "40", "100", "200"), name = "Sample Size Total")#+
#scale_x_discrete(limits=c(0.5, 2), breaks = c(0.5,1,2), labels = c(".5","1","2")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=c(5, 50, 100), minor_breaks=NULL)+   #seq(-3,2,0.5)
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=c(.01, .03, .05), minor_breaks=NULL)   #seq(-3,2,0.5)
 # scale_shape_manual(#values=c(21, 24, 22, 23), name="Sample size n1")#+
 # scale_color_manual(name="Method", #values=c("#F8766D", "#00BA38"),# "#619CFF"), 
 #                    labels=c("aWd*", "t-test"))#+
 
png(paste0(output_dir, (chunk_plot_name),".png"), width=12, height=12, units="in", res=400)#, width=18, height=14)
print(chunk_plot)
## Warning: Removed 410 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_text()`).
## Removed 410 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_text()`).
dev.off()

##                 2
#dev.off(dev.list()["png"])
print(chunk_plot)
